codeReview.summary: The AI inventory tools implementation is well-structured and follows existing codebase patterns. Two VoltAgent tools were created: check_inventory for querying stock levels by product name/SKU/barcode, and get_product_info for retrieving detailed product information including price, description, and availability. The implementation properly integrates with WhatsApp through the existing agent architecture.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the existing pattern of instantiating repositories directly in tool files rather than using dependency injection. This is consistent with how other AI chat tools are implemented (services.ts, patient.ts, appointments.ts all use `new Repository()` at module level).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/services/ai/chat/tools/inventory.ts:8-9 - const productRepository = new ProductRepository(); const inventoryRepository = new InventoryRepository(); matches pattern in services.ts:5, patient.ts:8-9, etc.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly added 'Direct' suffix methods (searchByNameOrSkuDirect, getStockDirect, findByProductIdDirect) to repositories for AI tool usage, bypassing the RequestContext requirement. This follows the pattern established in the codebase for tools that need direct profileId-based access.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/services/repository/product.ts:147-183 - searchByNameOrSkuDirect method added. packages/api/src/services/repository/inventory.ts:88-102 - findByProductIdDirect method exists. packages/api/src/services/repository/inventory.ts:180-195 - getStockDirect method exists.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill procedure was followed correctly. The worker invoked the fullstack-backend skill, read existing patterns, created the inventory tools, added necessary repository methods, and registered tools with the agent. TypeScript compilation passed successfully.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Skill invocation, pattern exploration (reading services.ts, product.ts schema), implementation, typecheck passed with no inventory/product related errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly uses Spanish for user-facing messages in the tool responses (e.g., 'No se encontraron productos', 'Stock bajo', 'Disponible', 'Agotado'), following the project convention that user-facing text should be in Spanish.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/services/ai/chat/tools/inventory.ts:63 - Spanish message for no products found. Line 96 - '⚠️ Stock bajo'. Line 215-217 - '✅ Disponible' and '❌ Agotado'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tools properly handle error states with try-catch blocks and return structured error responses that the AI agent can interpret and communicate to users.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/services/ai/chat/tools/inventory.ts:102-109 - Error handling in checkInventoryTool. Lines 220-227 - Error handling in getProductInfoTool. Both return { error: true, message: ... } structure.

summary: Feature m7-1-ai-inventory-tools has been successfully implemented. The worker created two VoltAgent tools (check_inventory and get_product_info) that enable WhatsApp conversations to query inventory data. The implementation follows existing patterns, properly integrates with the repository layer, handles errors gracefully, and uses Spanish for user-facing messages. No blocking or non-blocking issues were found. The feature fulfills VAL-AI-001 (Inventory query via WhatsApp) and VAL-AI-002 (Product information query) from the validation contract.
